regime_justification: USDJPY holding near MOF watch line (159.38), BOJ on hold but leaning dovish, vol subdued, carry trade stable yet MOF signaling intervention readiness

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY 18 pips from 160, MOF Katayama issued fresh intervention warning on April 24 citing 'decisive action with US coordination', BOJ April meeting Monday likely holds with Iran uncertainty cited

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ hold Monday (April 27–28) with dovish Iran hedge keeps carry intact but MOF rhetoric and 160 proximity elevate intervention risk; vol low

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Post-BOJ positioning period; if no intervention by May 10 and USDJPY respects 160, stage remains yellow; any MOF rate-check moves grade to B

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: Mid-May window: Iran ceasefire extended but supply shock persists; spread steady (186 bps); no near-term catalyst for forced unwind unless intervention executed

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: June BOJ (June 15–16) offers fresh normalization signal and Iran situation clarifies; absent April-May shock, unwind risk recedes into summer

bottom_line: Carry trade structurally intact—spread 186 bps, vol 7.7%, MOVE 67—but USDJPY 18 pips from 160 triggers MOF alert mode. BOJ holds Monday citing Iran fog, keeps 75 bps on hold. Risk is intervention, not organic unwind. If MOF acts, grade flips to B overnight. Otherwise, yellow persists until mid-May clarity.

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl > prior peak
trigger_map.note: ¥/bbl 15,050; WTI pulled back April 24 on Pakistan mediation hopes but Hormuz closed; Iran ceasefire extended but supply shock persists

narrative.p1: USDJPY closed April 25 at 159.38, 18 pips below the 160.00 MOF intervention watch line. Finance Minister Katayama issued a fresh intervention warning April 24, citing 'decisive action' in coordination with the US—the sharpest rhetoric since the January NY Fed rate-check episode. WTI crude pulled back 1.5% to $94.40 on hopes of a Pakistan-mediated US-Iran breakthrough, easing imported-inflation pressure temporarily. Nikkei 225 hit a record close at 59,716 (+0.97%), powered by tech earnings, while JGB 10Y climbed to 2.44% (one-week high) on March core CPI uptick to 1.8% and energy cost concerns. VIX and MOVE remain subdued (18.71 / 67.0), signaling no cross-asset stress. Regime remains yellow (unchanged from April 25): carry trade structurally intact but USDJPY proximity to 160 and MOF signaling place us in Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention).
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP 10Y spread 186 bps (4.31% − 2.44%), down 11 bps but still wide. USDJPY 1M implied vol 7.7% (April 17 data), carry-to-vol proxy ~24× (186 / 7.7), historically stable. USDJPY 5-day change −0.13%, pace benign. Distance from 60-day mean: neutral to slightly stretched. BOJ meets Monday April 27–28; consensus hold at 0.75% after Bloomberg April 21 sources cited Iran uncertainty as reason to pause. Ueda's April 13 speech (delivered by Himino) emphasized 'Middle East developments remain uncertain,' cooling April hike expectations. June 15–16 is the next live meeting. MOF rhetoric is the dominant near-term risk, not organic carry unwind. If Katayama intervenes or conducts rate-checks, 1-week grade flips to B instantly. Absent that, the trade persists in yellow. Applied unwind prior at 2-week horizon: data is mixed (vol low, spread stable, but MOF cocked and JGB rising), so tiebreaker leans C instead of D. Would otherwise grade D if MOF rhetoric were absent.
narrative.p3: BTC at $77,430 (−0.29% 24h), consolidating mid-70s after failing to reclaim $80k. Risk-on bid fading but no capitulation. Crypto liquidity neutral: BTC open interest direction and DXY correlation data unavailable, but price action suggests rotation out of risk assets is orderly, not forced. XRP spot data not retrieved. If carry unwinds (intervention or BOJ surprise), expect BTC to test $72k support as levered longs deleverage. For now, crypto is a lagging indicator—equities (Nikkei record, S&P near highs April 24) and stable vol (VIX 18.71) suggest risk appetite intact. Crypto positioning: hold core, trim leverage, watch for intervention headline as signal to reduce exposure. Unwind would drain crypto liquidity faster than equities due to thinner order books.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: 1-week C driven by BOJ April 27–28 consensus hold (leaning dovish on Iran uncertainty per April 21 Bloomberg, Ueda April 13 speech) and MOF intervention risk (Katayama April 24 warning, USDJPY 18 pips from 160). If BOJ delivers dovish hold and MOF refrains from action, 2-week remains C. If MOF intervenes or rate-checks by May 3, 2-week upgrades to B as market reprices carry viability. Spread and vol stable, so organic unwind unlikely absent policy shock.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: 2-week C holds into 3-week C if April 27–May 10 window sees no intervention and USDJPY respects 160 ceiling. Iran ceasefire extended through mid-May but Hormuz supply shock persists; oil volatility (WTI pulled back April 24 but remains elevated) injects tail risk. JGB 10Y at 2.44% approaches 2.5% prior peak; break above 2.5% by mid-May would tighten financial conditions and edge closer to Stage 3. No near-term BOJ catalyst (next meeting June 15–16), so 3-week risk is intervention or exogenous energy spike, not policy normalization.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: 3-week C downgrades to 4-week D as June BOJ meeting (June 15–16) approaches and Iran situation clarifies (ceasefire expires or extends). If MOF has not intervened by late May, market concludes 160 is tolerated and carry resumes with lower intervention premium. June BOJ may signal normalization (hike or hawkish guidance) but unlikely to shock given March hold and April hold expected. Absent April-May intervention or exogenous shock, unwind risk recedes into summer as spread remains wide (186 bps), vol low (7.7%), and earnings season supports equities (Nikkei record). 4-week D reflects base case of policy inertia and energy situation stabilizing, not bullish carry resumption.

watch_list: BOJ decision April 28: hold expected, but any hawkish tilt (rate-hike signal for June) or dovish surprise (committing to hold through summer) moves 1-week grade instantly
MOF intervention or rate-check headlines April 26–May 3: USDJPY 18 pips from 160, Katayama rhetoric escalating; any MOF action flips 1-week to B
JGB 10Y break above 2.50%: currently 2.44%; prior cycle high ~2.5%; break signals tighter financial conditions and edges toward Stage 3
WTI crude direction: April 24 pull-back on Pakistan mediation hopes; sustained move below $90 eases ¥/bbl pressure and lowers intervention risk; move above $100 reignites energy shock tail
USDJPY 1M vol or risk reversals: currently 7.7% IV; any spike above 10% or JPY-call skew flip signals market pricing intervention or unwind risk
